topic: Claude Mythos vs GPT-6 2026: Frontier Model Showdown for Developers

key_points: BenchLM aggregate score: Claude Mythos Preview 99 vs GPT-5.5 91
Coding average: Mythos 83.8 vs GPT-5.5 58.6
Agentic tasks: Mythos 82.4 vs GPT-5.5 81.5
Multimodal: Mythos 92.4 vs GPT-5.5 70.4
Mythos leads SWE-bench Verified (93.9% vs 88.7%) and SWE-bench Pro (77.8% vs 58.6%)
GPT-5.5 leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 (82.7% vs 82.0%)
GPT-5.5 is ~5x cheaper: $5/$30 vs estimated $25/$125 per 1M tokens
GPT-5.5 is publicly available; Mythos is invitation-only via Project Glasswing
GPT-5.5 (codename 'Spud') released April 23, 2026 — the de facto 'GPT-6' OpenAI delivered
GPT-5.5 narrowly beats Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0
Claude Mythos is restricted to ~40 companies in Project Glasswing security coalition
Industry consensus: pick Mythos for hardest coding; GPT-5.5 for accessible agentic workflows
Claude Mythos has unprecedented cybersecurity capabilities — zero-days in every major OS and browser
Mythos found 17-year-old RCE vulnerability in FreeBSD (CVE-2026-4747) autonomously
GPT-5.5 has 60% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.4
GPT-5.5 ships in 3 variants: standard, Thinking, Pro
GPT-5.5 available in ChatGPT, Codex CLI, and API from day one
Standard pricing: $5/M input, $30/M output; Pro: $30/$180
GPT-5.5 Thinking and Pro variants offer deeper reasoning
Multi-model routing is now best practice — no single model wins everything

stats.fact: Claude Mythos Preview achieved 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified — the highest score ever recorded by any AI model, leading 89 evaluated models
GPT-5.5 achieved 88.7% on SWE-bench Verified and 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 at launch (April 23, 2026)
Claude Mythos leads SWE-bench Pro at 77.8% vs GPT-5.5's 58.6% — a 19-point gap for real-world production codebases
Claude Mythos solves GitHub issues from production codebases correctly 19 out of 20 times (SWE-bench Verified 93.9%)
GPT-5.5 API pricing: $5/M input, $30/M output — roughly 4.2x cheaper than Mythos's estimated $25/$125 on output
Anthropic committed $100M in model usage credits to Project Glasswing, which restricts Claude Mythos to ~40 partner companies for defensive cybersecurity
Claude Mythos identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across every major OS and browser; over 99% remain unpatched
GPT-5.5 released April 23, 2026 — the model widely expected to launch as 'GPT-6' was versioned as 5.5 instead
BenchLM aggregate ranking: Claude Mythos Preview scores 99 vs GPT-5.5's 91 across all evaluated benchmarks
In 18 months, the AI frontier went from a 2-horse race to 22 competing frontier models as of mid-2026
